tools.name: create_input_action
tools.description: Create a UInputAction data asset (IA_*). This is an Enhanced Input action asset that defines a conceptual input command (e.g., IA_Jump, IA_Move, IA_Look, IA_ToggleCamera). The value_type determines the data shape: Boolean for digital buttons, Axis1D for single-axis (triggers), Axis2D for 2D movement/look, Axis3D for spatial input.

asset_path.description: Full content path for the new Input Action asset (e.g., /Game/ThirdPerson/Input/Actions/IA_ToggleCamera)

value_type.description: Input value type: Boolean (digital on/off), Axis1D (float), Axis2D (Vector2D), Axis3D (Vector). Default: Boolean

consume_input.description: Whether this action consumes the input, preventing it from reaching other actions. Default: true

tools.name: create_input_mapping_context
tools.description: Create a UInputMappingContext data asset (IMC_*). This is an Enhanced Input mapping context that holds a collection of key-to-action bindings. Mapping contexts are swappable at runtime and have priorities to control which bindings take precedence.

asset_path.description: Full content path for the new Input Mapping Context asset (e.g., /Game/ThirdPerson/Input/IMC_Default)

tools.name: add_input_mapping
tools.description: Add a hardware key binding to an existing Input Mapping Context, mapping a specific key to an Input Action. Optionally attach input modifiers (Negate, Swizzle, DeadZone, Scalar) and triggers (Pressed, Released, Hold, Tap, Pulse) to the binding.

mapping_context_path.description: Content path of the Input Mapping Context to modify (e.g., /Game/ThirdPerson/Input/IMC_Default)

action_path.description: Content path of the Input Action to bind (e.g., /Game/ThirdPerson/Input/Actions/IA_ToggleCamera)

key.description: Hardware key name (e.g., C, SpaceBar, W, S, A, D, LeftMouseButton, RightMouseButton, Gamepad_FaceButton_Bottom, Mouse2D, Gamepad_LeftThumbstick_2D)

modifiers.description: Optional list of input modifiers to apply to this mapping

triggers.description: Optional list of input triggers to apply to this mapping. If omitted, the default trigger behavior applies (Pressed).
